---
title: "Analyzing US Census Data"
author: "Pushpendu Biswas"
format: html
execute:
  echo: false
---

```{r}
#| message: false
library(tidyverse)
library(tidycensus)
library(sf)
```

```{r}
#| message: false
income_tx <- get_acs(
  geography = "county",
  variables = "B19013_001",
  state = "TX",
  year = 2020,
  geometry = TRUE
)
```

```{r}
income_tx |>
  ggplot(aes(fill = estimate)) +
  geom_sf() +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(labels = scales::label_dollar()) +
  theme_void() +
  labs(
    title = "Median Household Income in Texas Counties",
    fill = "Median Income",
    caption = "Source: 2020 American Community Survey (ACS), US Census Bureau"
  )
```

```{r}
#| cache: true
#| message: false
edu_state <- get_acs(
  geography = "state",
  variables = c(
    "B15003_022",
    "B15003_023",
    "B15003_024",
    "B15003_025"
  ),
  summary_var = "B15003_001",
  year = 2020
)
```

```{r}
#| fig-height: 10
edu_state |>
  group_by(GEOID, NAME) |>
  summarize(
    pct_bachelors = sum(estimate, na.rm = TRUE) / first(summary_est) * 100,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  mutate(NAME = fct_reorder(NAME, pct_bachelors)) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = pct_bachelors, y = NAME)) +
  geom_col(fill = "steelblue") +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::label_percent(scale = 1), expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.05))) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title.position = "plot",
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank()
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Educational Attainment by State",
    subtitle = "Percentage of adults aged 25+ with at least a bachelor's degree",
    x = "Percentage of Adults",
    y = NULL,
    caption = "Source: 2020 American Community Survey (ACS), US Census Bureau"
  )
```

```{r}
#| message: false
age_ca <- get_acs(
  geography = "county",
  variables = c(
    median_age = "B01002_001",
    population = "B01003_001"
  ),
  state = "CA",
  year = 2020,
  geometry = FALSE
)
```

```{r}
age_ca |>
  select(GEOID, NAME, variable, estimate) |>
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = variable,
    values_from = estimate
  ) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = population, y = median_age)) +
  geom_point(color = "steelblue", alpha = 0.7, size = 2) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = ifelse(population > 1.5e6, str_remove(NAME, " County, California"), "")),
    nudge_y = 0.4,
    size = 3,
    hjust = 0.5
  ) +
  scale_x_log10(labels = scales::label_comma()) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "California Counties: Population vs. Median Age",
    subtitle = "Population shown on a logarithmic scale; largest counties labeled",
    x = "Total Population (log scale)",
    y = "Median Age",
    caption = "Source: 2020 American Community Survey (ACS), US Census Bureau"
  )
```
